analysis.overall_assessment: Your autonomic function shows concerning volatility with HRV swinging from 40 to 90 ms across the week, though your excellent resting HR control (45-52 bpm) suggests good baseline POTS management. Sleep quality remains consistently strong (81-92 scores) with adequate deep sleep for immune recovery, and stable temperature deviations indicate well-controlled Hashimoto's activity currently.

analysis.autonomic_assessment: Your autonomic system shows excellent baseline control with consistently low resting HR (45-52 bpm), but concerning HRV instability ranging from 40-90 ms suggests ongoing sympathetic nervous system volatility. The pattern indicates POTS is well-managed at rest, but autonomic recovery capacity fluctuates significantly day-to-day, particularly following shorter sleep duration.
analysis.thyroid_immune_signals: Temperature deviations remain stable at 0.0°C average with no spikes, suggesting well-controlled Hashimoto's activity currently. Deep sleep duration of 1.2-2.0 hours nightly provides adequate immune recovery time, indicating your supplement protocol is effectively supporting thyroid autoimmune management.
analysis.vestibular_readiness: Recovery metrics indicate good capacity for vestibular rehab most days, but the readiness drop to 72 on 4/25 with HRV at 40 ms suggests scaling back vestibular exercises on such low-recovery days. Your nervous system appears to have sufficient capacity for rehabilitation when HRV is above 60 ms.
analysis.focus_for_next_week: Prioritize sleep consistency above 7 hours nightly to stabilize HRV volatility, as the data shows shorter sleep directly precedes autonomic instability that compromises both vestibular compensation and POTS management.
